mnemonics: GRAPHS: G = Graph, R = Relationships, A = Adjacency, P = Paths, H = Hierarchies, S = Structures
keyPoints: Graphs consist of vertices (nodes) and edges (connections)
Common graph representations include adjacency matrices and adjacency lists
Graph algorithms include traversal methods like BFS and DFS, and pathfinding algorithms like Dijkstra's
commonMistakes: Using incorrect data structures for graph representation (e.g., using adjacency matrix for sparse graphs)
Forgetting to mark visited nodes during traversal leading to infinite loops
